nl_query: Detect more than 5 consecutive failed login attempts for the same user account within 5 minutes
Alert on failed SSH login attempts from a single source IP exceeding 10 failures within 5 minutes
Detect Windows event ID 4625 logon type 3 (network) exceeding 15 failures per source IP in 5 minutes
Alert on a password spray attack: one source IP failing authentication against more than 20 distinct accounts within 10 minutes
Detect impossible travel: same user account authenticating successfully from two different countries within 90 minutes
Alert on successful login within 60 seconds after five or more failed logons for the same account (event ID 4624 after 4625)
Detect RDP brute-force: more than 20 failed Windows event 4625 logon type 10 events from one external IP in 10 minutes
Alert on Kerberos pre-authentication failure event ID 4771 for more than 10 distinct accounts from the same source within 5 minutes
Detect credential-stuffing success: successful login from a source IP that accumulated more than 50 failed attempts across different accounts in the preceding hour
Alert when a service account authenticates interactively (logon type 2) outside of approved maintenance windows
Detect NTLM authentication downgrade: event ID 4776 failures from hosts that have previously used Kerberos
Alert when a user account logs on from more than 5 distinct source IPs within a 30-minute window
Detect disabled account logon attempts: Windows event ID 4625 with failure reason 0xC0000072
Alert on MFA bypass: successful authentication event without a corresponding MFA approval event within the same session
Detect password change immediately followed by a logon from a new geolocation within 15 minutes for the same account
Alert on Windows event ID 4648 (explicit credential use) where the target account differs from the logged-on user
Detect password spray against Azure AD: more than 30 failed sign-ins with error code AADSTS50126 from a single IP across more than 15 unique accounts in 10 minutes
Alert on guest account authentication to domain resources: event ID 4624 where account name ends with '$' and logon type is 3
Detect logon outside business hours: successful authentication events between 22:00 and 06:00 for non-service accounts
Alert on Kerberoasting activity: more than 5 TGS requests (event ID 4769) with encryption type 0x17 (RC4) for service accounts within 1 minute from a single user
Detect DNS queries to newly registered domains (registered within the last 30 days) from internal hosts
Alert on DNS tunneling: single host generating more than 100 DNS queries per minute with average query length exceeding 50 characters
Detect beaconing behaviour: an internal host making outbound HTTP connections to the same external IP at regular intervals (jitter less than 5%) over a 6-hour window
Alert on large outbound data transfers: more than 500 MB sent to a single external IP in one hour over port 443
Detect internal host communicating directly with a known Tor entry or exit node IP address
Alert on port scanning: a single internal host initiating connections to more than 100 distinct destination ports on more than 10 different hosts within 5 minutes
Detect ICMP tunneling: ICMP echo requests from a single host exceeding 1 MB total payload in 10 minutes or with payload size consistently above 100 bytes
Alert on non-standard port usage: HTTP traffic (user-agent present) detected on ports other than 80, 443, 8080, or 8443
Detect outbound connections to IP addresses categorised as malicious in threat intelligence feeds within the last 24 hours
Alert on SMB lateral movement: a single host establishing SMB connections (port 445) to more than 20 distinct internal hosts within 5 minutes
Detect domain generation algorithm (DGA) activity: DNS queries with high entropy names (Shannon entropy > 3.5) returning NXDOMAIN more than 20 times per hour from one host
Alert on cleartext credential transmission: HTTP POST requests containing fields named password or passwd to external destinations
Detect network reconnaissance: ARP requests from a single host to more than 50 unique IP addresses within the same subnet in under 2 minutes
Alert when an internal host resolves a domain via DNS over HTTPS (DoH) to a provider not in the approved list
Detect fast-flux DNS: a domain returning more than 10 distinct A records within a 5-minute window with TTL under 60 seconds
Alert on outbound connections from servers to external IPs on ephemeral ports (above 49151) that are not part of an established session
Detect IRC command-and-control: outbound TCP connections to port 6667 or 6697 carrying PRIVMSG or JOIN commands in payload
Alert on protocol anomaly: TLS handshake with a JA3 fingerprint matching known malware families detected on egress traffic
Detect LLMNR or NBT-NS poisoning attempts: unsolicited LLMNR responses from hosts that did not originate the query
Alert on unexpected outbound SMTP: any internal host other than approved mail servers initiating outbound connections on port 25 or 587
Detect cmd.exe or powershell.exe spawned as a child process of a browser process such as chrome.exe, firefox.exe, or msedge.exe
Alert on PowerShell encoded command execution: processes with command line containing -EncodedCommand or -enc with a Base64 payload longer than 100 characters
Detect process hollowing: a process where the image on disk differs from the in-memory image hash, identified via Sysmon event ID 25 or equivalent telemetry
Alert on suspicious parent-child process relationship: wscript.exe or cscript.exe spawning cmd.exe or powershell.exe
Detect execution of binaries from user-writable directories: processes launched from %APPDATA%, %TEMP%, or %PUBLIC%
Alert on LSASS memory access: a non-system process opening a handle to lsass.exe with access rights including PROCESS_VM_READ (0x0010)
Detect living-off-the-land binary abuse: certutil.exe executed with -decode, -urlcache, or -encode arguments
Alert on process injection via CreateRemoteThread: Sysmon event ID 8 where source and target process images belong to different users or integrity levels
Detect mshta.exe executing remote content: mshta.exe command line containing http:// or https:// URLs
Alert on suspicious scheduled task creation via schtasks.exe with a command line referencing a remote URL or UNC path
Detect indirect command execution via pcalua.exe, forfiles.exe, or syncappvpublishingserver.exe used to proxy execution of arbitrary binaries
Alert on WMI process creation: wmiprvse.exe spawning cmd.exe, powershell.exe, or any binary outside of system32
Detect execution of renamed system binaries: a process with a name not matching known system tools but sharing the same hash as a known Windows binary
Alert on reflective DLL injection: a process loading a DLL that has no corresponding file on disk as detected by loaded module path being empty or pointing to a memory address range
Detect regsvr32.exe used for script execution: command line containing /s and /u flags with a remote URL (Squiblydoo technique)
Alert on execution of scripting engines from unusual locations: wscript.exe or cscript.exe running scripts outside of %SystemRoot%
Detect parent PID spoofing: a process whose reported parent PID does not match the actual creating process as observed through kernel callback telemetry
Alert on suspicious use of rundll32.exe: execution with comsvcs.dll and MiniDump or with JavaScript entry points
Detect cmd.exe /c or powershell.exe -c with a pipe character redirecting output to a network location or clip.exe
Alert on token impersonation: a process performing SeImpersonatePrivilege usage outside of services.exe, iis worker processes, or SQL Server service accounts
Detect new registry run key creation under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run or HKLM equivalent by a non-system process
Alert on scheduled task creation (event ID 4698) where the task action references a path in %TEMP%, %APPDATA%, or a UNC path
Detect WMI event subscription persistence: creation of __EventFilter, __EventConsumer, or __FilterToConsumerBinding objects in the root\subscription namespace
Alert on new Windows service installation (event ID 7045) where the service binary path points to a user-writable or temporary directory
Detect modification of the Windows Startup folder: file creation or modification events in C:\Users\*\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup
Alert on DLL search order hijacking: a DLL with the same name as a known system DLL being created in a directory that appears earlier in the PATH than System32
Detect SSH authorized_keys modification: write events to any ~/.ssh/authorized_keys file on Linux hosts by a process other than sshd or a sanctioned provisioning tool
Alert on cron job modification: file write events to /etc/cron.d/, /etc/cron.daily/, /etc/crontab, or user crontab changes by a non-root non-scheduled process
Detect bootkit or MBR modification: raw disk write to sector 0 of a physical disk by any process other than a signed disk management utility
Alert on suspicious LSA package registration: changes to HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\Security Packages or Authentication Packages registry values
Detect new user account creation outside of the provisioning system: Windows event ID 4720 not preceded by a corresponding ITSM ticket or provisioning API event within 5 minutes
Alert on COM object hijacking: creation of HKCU\Software\Classes\CLSID entries that shadow existing HKLM CLSID registrations used by privileged processes
Detect modification of Windows accessibility feature binaries (sethc.exe, utilman.exe, osk.exe) or their Image File Execution Options registry keys
Alert on new startup service with automatic start type that is not in the organisation's approved service catalogue
Detect firmware persistence: unexpected write access to UEFI variable storage or ESP partition by a user-mode process without a signed firmware update framework
Detect sudo usage for commands not in the approved sudoers policy: Linux audit log events showing sudo execution of binaries not listed in /etc/sudoers
Alert on token privilege escalation: Windows event ID 4672 (special privileges assigned) for accounts that are not members of privileged groups
Detect UAC bypass via fodhelper.exe: process creation of fodhelper.exe followed within 30 seconds by a child process running at high integrity level
Alert on SetUID binary execution on Linux: execve system calls for files with the setuid bit set from a process whose effective UID does not match the file owner
Detect addition of a standard user account to local Administrators or Domain Admins group: Windows event ID 4732 or 4728
Alert on named pipe impersonation: a process creating a named pipe with a name matching known Windows system pipe patterns and then calling ImpersonateNamedPipeClient
Detect DCOM object abuse for local privilege escalation: activation of high-privilege DCOM servers (e.g., WBEM, ShellBrowserWindow) by a medium-integrity process
Alert on Group Policy modification by a non-Domain Admin account: event ID 5136 in the directory service log where the object class is groupPolicyContainer
Detect kernel exploit attempt: a user-mode process invoking NtAllocateVirtualMemory to map memory at address 0x0 (NULL dereference setup) or using unusual kernel object type manipulation syscalls
Alert on abuse of SeDebugPrivilege: a process acquiring SeDebugPrivilege outside of known debuggers, task managers, or endpoint agents
Detect runAs or runas.exe usage to execute processes under a different domain account on an endpoint
Alert on PrintSpoofer or similar impersonation exploit: spoolsv.exe spawning a child process running under SYSTEM context other than known legitimate print processor binaries
Detect container privilege escalation: a Docker container process creating files owned by UID 0 on the host filesystem via a volume mount
Alert on Active Directory ACL abuse: modification of AdminSDHolder object permissions or protected group membership by a non-Domain Admin account
Detect DCShadow attack: a non-DC host registering itself as a domain controller by creating nTDSDSA objects in Active Directory (event IDs 4662 and 5137 on schema partition)
Alert on Azure role assignment elevation: a user granting themselves Owner or Contributor role on a subscription or resource group without a corresponding PIM request
Detect pkexec or polkit invocation on Linux by a non-root user that results in a root-privileged process within 5 seconds
Alert on ADCS certificate template abuse: enrollment in a certificate template with EKU allowing client authentication and with ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT flag set by a standard user account
Detect Juicy Potato or similar token impersonation: process creating a COM server stub then immediately calling CreateProcessWithTokenW from a low-privilege service account
Alert on unexpected sudoers file modification: write event to /etc/sudoers or files under /etc/sudoers.d/ not originating from an authorised configuration management process
Detect execution of net.exe or net1.exe with group, user, localgroup, or accounts arguments on an endpoint
Alert on Active Directory enumeration: ldap queries from a non-DC host requesting all user objects or all computer objects from the directory within a 5-minute window
Detect BloodHound-style AD reconnaissance: a single account performing more than 500 LDAP queries involving memberOf, adminCount, or servicePrincipalName attributes within 10 minutes
Alert on network share enumeration: a single host making more than 20 IPC$ connections (event ID 5140) to distinct remote hosts within 5 minutes
Detect whoami, hostname, or ipconfig /all execution on Windows endpoints, particularly when spawned from an interpreter or scripting engine
Alert on cloud metadata service access from a workload that has not previously queried the instance metadata endpoint (169.254.169.254 or fd00:ec2::254)
Detect internal network scanning: nmap or masscan process execution, or raw TCP SYN packets sent to more than 50 distinct internal IPs from one host within 2 minutes
Alert on system information discovery: systeminfo.exe or uname -a combined with hostname and whoami executed sequentially within 60 seconds on the same host
Detect Active Directory trust enumeration: nltest.exe /domain_trusts or Get-ADTrust PowerShell cmdlet execution by a non-administrative account
Alert on security product discovery: tasklist, sc query, or Get-Process commands that reference known AV or EDR product names (MsMpEng, CrowdStrike, SentinelOne)
Detect route or arp -a execution on an endpoint, especially when combined with other reconnaissance commands within a 2-minute window
Alert on AWS IAM enumeration: a single IAM principal calling ListUsers, ListRoles, ListPolicies, and GetAccountAuthorizationDetails within a 10-minute window without a corresponding service ticket
Detect file system enumeration: dir /s /b or find / -type f executed on large directory trees generating more than 10,000 file stat events within 60 seconds
Alert on browser history or credential store access: a non-browser process reading files in Chrome's or Firefox's profile directory containing Login Data or key4.db
Detect Kerberos SPN scanning: a single user account requesting TGS tickets for more than 10 unique service principal names within 1 minute (precursor to Kerberoasting)
Alert on virtual machine or sandbox detection attempts: process querying registry keys HKLM\SOFTWARE\VMware or CPUID leaf 0x40000000 via unusual API sequences
Detect process list enumeration via tasklist.exe or ps aux executed by a web server worker process or application service account
Alert on subnet sweep using ICMP: more than 200 ICMP echo requests to sequential IP addresses within the same /24 subnet sent from one host within 30 seconds
Detect GCP project enumeration: gcloud projects list or equivalent API calls to resourcemanager.projects.list by a service account not authorised for cross-project access
Alert on Local Security Authority policy query: lsapolicylookup or auditpol /get /category:* executed by a standard user account
Detect LSASS dump via procdump.exe: procdump.exe executed with -ma or -mm flags targeting lsass.exe
Alert on LSASS memory read via MiniDumpWriteDump API: any non-system process calling MiniDumpWriteDump with a handle to lsass.exe as the process parameter
Detect Mimikatz execution patterns: process command line or loaded modules matching sekurlsa::, lsadump::, or kerberos:: tokens
Alert on SAM database access: any non-system process opening a handle to HKLM\SAM or attempting to read C:\Windows\System32\config\SAM
Detect DCSync attack: a non-DC account performing replication directory changes (GetNCChanges) as detected by event ID 4662 with rights GUID 1131f6aa or 1131f6ad
Alert on credential manager access: vaultcmd.exe or Windows Vault API calls from a process other than approved credential management tools
Detect /etc/shadow read access on Linux: any process other than passwd, shadow, or PAM libraries opening /etc/shadow for reading

Alert on NTDS.dit extraction: volume shadow copy creation (vssadmin, wmic shadowcopy, or PowerShell New-ShadowCopy) followed within 5 minutes by file copy from the VSS snapshot path containing ntds.dit
Detect keylogger installation: creation of a low-level keyboard hook via SetWindowsHookEx with WH_KEYBOARD_LL by a non-accessibility process
Alert on clipboard data theft: a process calling OpenClipboard and GetClipboardData more than 10 times within 60 seconds without being a known clipboard manager
Detect AS-REP Roasting: event ID 4768 with pre-authentication type 0x0 (no pre-auth) for accounts where DONT_REQ_PREAUTH is not expected to be set
Alert on network password sniffing: tcpdump, wireshark, or tshark executed on an endpoint outside of an approved network monitoring window
Detect browser saved password access: a non-browser process reading Chrome's Login Data SQLite file or Firefox's logins.json while the browser is not running
Alert on over-pass-the-hash (pass-the-key): event ID 4768 (TGT request) using RC4 encryption from a host where the account's normal encryption type is AES256
Detect DPAPI master key access by non-system processes: reading files under %APPDATA%\Microsoft\Protect\* by processes outside of lsass.exe or credential manager APIs
Alert on cloud service credential file access: a process reading ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.azure/accessTokens.json, or application-default-credentials.json outside of approved SDK processes
Detect shadow credential attack: addition of msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute to a user or computer object by a non-privileged account (event ID 5136 on the user object)
Alert on SSH private key access: a process reading files named id_rsa, id_ecdsa, or id_ed25519 outside of the SSH client binary or configuration management tools
Detect password in command line: process creation events where the command line contains -password, --password, -p with a value, or similar credential-passing patterns
Alert on Zerologon exploitation attempt: Netlogon secure channel establishment requests with ComputerName matching a DC and ClientChallenge containing all-zero bytes (CVE-2020-1472)
Detect PsExec lateral movement: creation of PSEXESVC service (event ID 7045) on a remote host originating from a non-administrative workstation
Alert on WMI remote execution: wmiprvse.exe creating child processes on a host where the originating WMI call came from a remote non-management system
Detect pass-the-hash via SMB: event ID 4624 logon type 3 with NTLM authentication where the source workstation differs from the account's normal authentication patterns
Alert on administrative share access: a workstation account accessing ADMIN$, C$, or IPC$ shares on another workstation (not a server)
Detect Cobalt Strike lateral movement via service creation: a remote service being created whose binary path contains powershell.exe, cmd.exe /c, or a UNC path to a share
Alert on RDP lateral movement from unexpected sources: successful RDP logins (event ID 4624 logon type 10) to servers originating from workstations rather than jump hosts
Detect SSH hop chaining: an SSH connection to a bastion host immediately followed by a new SSH session originating from that bastion to an internal host within 30 seconds

Alert on DCOM-based lateral movement: spawning of processes through mmc.exe, Excel.exe, or other DCOM applications on a remote host as a result of inbound port 135 connections from a non-admin workstation
Detect PowerShell remoting lateral movement: wsmprovhost.exe spawning child processes on a host receiving inbound WinRM connections from non-jump-server sources
Alert on scheduled task created on a remote host: schtasks.exe execution with /s flag specifying a remote hostname from a non-admin workstation account
Detect Kerberos delegation abuse: a service account with unconstrained delegation receiving a forwardable TGT and immediately making a TGS request for a high-value service like CIFS on a DC
Alert on remote registry access: connection to WINREG named pipe (event ID 5145) from a non-administrative system, particularly to browse or write to HKLM hives
Detect net use commands targeting remote shares: net use * or net use \\hostname\share executed on an endpoint not performing backup or legitimate data access operations
Alert on Golden Ticket lateral movement: Kerberos ticket presented to a DC with a PAC signed by a krbtgt encryption key version (KVNO) that does not match the current or previous KVNO
Detect exploitation of SMB vulnerability: inbound SMB connections to port 445 with transaction data matching EternalBlue shellcode patterns (Sysmon network events combined with process creation)
Alert on remote file copy to Windows startup or system32 folder: xcopy, robocopy, or SMB write events depositing executable files in %SystemRoot%\System32 on a remote host

Detect Silver Ticket attack: Kerberos service ticket authentication where the PAC contains elevated privileges not matching the AD user object, identified by missing event ID 4768 (no TGT request) before 4769
Alert on atexec lateral movement: AT job or schtasks /create combined with a new scheduled task name matching random strings or short GUIDs on a remote host
Detect unusual inter-host PowerShell execution: Invoke-Command or Enter-PSSession targeting hosts outside of the approved remote management group
Alert on resource-based constrained delegation abuse: modification of msDS-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity attribute on a computer object by a non-Domain Admin account (event ID 5136)

Detect large file uploads to cloud storage: outbound HTTPS traffic to known storage endpoints (s3.amazonaws.com, blob.core.windows.net, drive.google.com) exceeding 100 MB per session from a single endpoint
Alert on DNS exfiltration: a host generating more than 50 unique DNS TXT record queries per minute to the same second-level domain with query names containing base64-encoded payloads
Detect email-based exfiltration: a single user sending more than 10 emails with attachments exceeding 5 MB to external recipients within 30 minutes
Alert on USB mass storage device data transfer: more than 1 GB of data written to a USB storage device on an endpoint within a single session
Detect HTTPS exfiltration via certificate pinning bypass: outbound TLS traffic with unusual handshake patterns (SNI mismatch, unusual cipher suites) to newly registered domains

Alert on archive and compress followed by immediate outbound transfer: 7z.exe, zip.exe, or tar execution producing an archive file, followed within 5 minutes by that file being accessed for an outbound network connection
Detect FTP or FTPS data transfer to external destinations from hosts that do not have an approved FTP use case in the asset inventory
Alert on steganographic exfiltration indicator: repeated upload of image files (JPEG, PNG) with file sizes consistently 5-20% larger than the baseline for that file type to an external cloud service
Detect bulk email forwarding rule creation: Exchange or Microsoft 365 audit event showing creation of an inbox rule that forwards all email to an external domain not in the corporate allowed list
Alert on SFTP or SCP session from a server to an external IP not in the approved data transfer allowlist

Detect exfiltration over alternative protocols: outbound traffic on port 53 (UDP and TCP) carrying payloads larger than 512 bytes without being legitimate DNS responses, sustained over more than 5 minutes
Alert on SharePoint or OneDrive mass download: a single user downloading more than 500 files within 30 minutes, deviating more than 3 standard deviations from their 30-day baseline
Detect print spooler-based exfiltration: large documents sent to network printers with an external IP, or print jobs to PDF redirected to network shares outside the organisation
Alert on covert channel exfiltration via HTTP headers: outbound HTTP requests with custom headers containing high-entropy strings (entropy > 4.0) sent repetitively to the same external host
Detect Slack or Teams-based exfiltration: a user uploading more than 50 files or 200 MB to a Slack workspace or Teams channel containing external guest members within one hour
Detect ransomware file extension changes: a process renaming or creating more than 100 files with new extensions not in the baseline within 60 seconds on any host
Alert on shadow copy deletion: vssadmin.exe, wmic.exe, or PowerShell executing Delete Shadows commands or Win32_ShadowCopy.Delete() method
Detect disk encryption activity: a process performing sequential write operations to raw disk sectors at a rate exceeding 50 MB/s outside of known backup windows
Alert on mass file deletion: a single process deleting more than 200 files within 30 seconds in directories outside its normal operating scope
Detect crypto-miner deployment: a process with high sustained CPU usage (>80% over 10 minutes) making outbound connections to known mining pool domains or Stratum protocol ports (3333, 4444, 8333)
Alert on website defacement attempt: unexpected write events to web server document root (htdocs, wwwroot, public_html) by a process other than the web deployment pipeline
Detect backup deletion: removal of backup catalog files or database backup sets (.bak, .bkf) by a process other than the authorised backup agent
Alert on wiper malware indicators: a process writing null bytes or fixed patterns to file content at scale, particularly targeting executables, documents, and databases simultaneously
Detect DDoS tool execution: LOIC, HOIC, hping3, or ab (Apache Bench) launched on endpoints not in a load testing or QA environment
Alert on system shutdown or restart initiated by a non-admin user or non-standard process: Windows event ID 1074 or shutdown.exe execution outside of patch management windows

Detect ransomware note creation: files named README.txt, DECRYPT_INSTRUCTIONS.html, or HOW_TO_RECOVER with content matching known ransom note patterns being created in multiple directories within 2 minutes
Alert on database destruction: DROP DATABASE or TRUNCATE TABLE statements executed by an application account against more than 3 databases within 5 minutes
Detect event log clearing: Windows event ID 1102 (Security audit log cleared) or 104 (System log cleared) generated by a non-SIEM or non-admin process
Alert on firmware destruction: a process writing crafted data to UEFI variable store or BMC firmware update interface outside of an approved maintenance window with a signed firmware package
Detect resource hijacking in cloud: sudden spike in compute instance creation or GPU-enabled VM deployment by an IAM principal whose baseline shows no prior compute provisioning activity
Detect AWS root account usage: CloudTrail event where userIdentity.type is Root for any API call other than account-level billing actions
Alert on S3 bucket policy modification that removes encryption requirements or adds a public access statement: S3:PutBucketPolicy event where the new policy grants s3:GetObject to Principal: '*'

Detect AWS credential exfiltration via metadata service: EC2 instance making GetCallerIdentity or ListRoles API calls with instance profile credentials from a process other than the expected application
Alert on Azure AD application consent grant to overly permissive OAuth scopes: admin consent granted for Directory.ReadWrite.All or Mail.ReadWrite by a non-privileged user via phishing consent flow
Detect CloudTrail logging disabled: StopLogging or DeleteTrail API calls in any AWS region, including regions not actively monitored

Alert on AWS privilege escalation via IAM policy attachment: iam:AttachUserPolicy, iam:AttachRolePolicy, or iam:PutUserPolicy called by a principal that did not previously have administrative privileges
Detect GCP service account key creation by a non-automated pipeline: iam.serviceAccountKeys.create called outside of the approved CI/CD service account
Alert on security group rule addition allowing 0.0.0.0/0 ingress on SSH (22), RDP (3389), or any port: AWS AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress or Azure NSG rule creation events

Detect AWS Lambda backdoor: a new Lambda function created with an IAM execution role that has AdministratorAccess, or an existing function's code updated to exfiltrate environment variables containing secrets

Alert on Azure storage blob public access enabled: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/write event setting allowBlobPublicAccess to true on a storage account containing sensitive data classifications
Detect AWS KMS key deletion scheduled: ScheduleKeyDeletion API call for a KMS key that is referenced by encrypted S3 buckets or EBS volumes
Alert on cloud persistence via Lambda or Azure Function scheduled trigger: creation of an event rule or timer trigger attached to a function that executes credential refresh or reverse shell payloads
Detect AWS GuardDuty suppression: CreateFilter, UpdateDetector with FindingPublishingFrequency set to SIX_HOURS, or DeleteDetector API calls by a non-security operations account
Alert on unusual cross-region API activity: AWS CloudTrail events originating from a region that has not been used by the account in the past 30 days

Detect SSRF-based metadata credential theft in cloud: web application access logs showing requests to 169.254.169.254 or fd00:ec2::254 that result in outbound API calls using the associated instance role within 5 minutes
Alert on Kubernetes secret enumeration: kubectl get secrets, kubectl describe secret, or API server audit log showing list/get operations on secrets resources by a non-privileged service account
Detect AWS IAM access key creation for a root account or a high-privilege role that previously used only console access
Alert on Azure PIM activation anomaly: privileged role activation request approved without MFA re-authentication, or activation from a new country not previously used by the requesting identity
Detect GCP org-level IAM binding modification: setIamPolicy called at the organisation or folder level granting roles/owner or roles/editor to an external identity outside the corporate domain

Alert on CloudWatch or Azure Monitor alert rule deletion: events matching DeleteAlerts, DeleteAlertRule, or DeleteMetricAlarm for security-relevant monitoring rules by a non-security operations account
Detect privileged container execution: Docker container started with --privileged flag or with SYS_ADMIN capability that is not in the approved privileged workload list
Alert on container escape via host path mount: a container mounting the host root filesystem (/) or /proc/1/root as a volume
Detect Kubernetes pod with hostNetwork and hostPID enabled: audit events showing pod creation spec with both hostNetwork: true and hostPID: true outside of DaemonSet system components
Alert on container image pull from untrusted registry: Docker or containerd pulling images from registries not in the organisation's approved image registry allowlist
Detect shell execution inside a running container: docker exec or kubectl exec events creating an interactive shell (bash, sh, or zsh) in a production container

Alert on Kubernetes RBAC privilege escalation: a service account binding to ClusterRole with verb '*' on resource '*', or creation of a ClusterRoleBinding granting cluster-admin to a non-system identity
Detect container breakout attempt via /proc/sysrq-trigger or /dev/mem access from within a container process
Alert on crypto-mining in containers: container process with sustained CPU usage above 90% making outbound connections to Stratum protocol endpoints or known mining pool domains
Detect Kubernetes API server credential theft: a pod mounting a service account token and making API calls to list secrets, configmaps, or pods across namespaces outside of its defined RBAC policy
Alert on container image with critical CVE running in production: image scan results showing CVSS >= 9.0 vulnerability in a container deployed to the production namespace within the last 24 hours
Detect modification of container runtime socket: any process other than the container runtime daemon writing to /var/run/docker.sock or /run/containerd/containerd.sock
Alert on Kubernetes etcd direct access: connections to etcd port 2379 or 2380 from a pod or host that is not an authorised control plane component
Detect supply chain attack via malicious base image: container image layers containing newly added setuid binaries or backdoor scripts not present in the official upstream image digest
Alert on namespace privilege boundary violation: a container process in one Kubernetes namespace making API calls to resources in a different namespace using a cross-namespace service account token

Detect container runtime exploitation via runc escape: a container process invoking openat or unlinkat syscalls targeting /proc/self/exe with the runtime binary path, indicative of CVE-2019-5736 pattern
Detect SQL injection attempts: web access logs showing requests with URL-encoded single quotes, UNION SELECT, or OR 1=1 patterns in query parameters
Alert on directory traversal attack: HTTP requests containing ../ or ..%2F sequences in the URL path targeting files outside the web root

Detect web shell upload and execution: HTTP POST to an upload endpoint returning a 200 response, followed within 5 minutes by a GET request to the uploaded file path that triggers a child process on the web server
Alert on cross-site scripting (XSS) attack: HTTP requests containing <script>, javascript:, or on* event handler patterns in user-supplied input fields reaching the application layer
Detect vulnerability scanning: a single source IP sending more than 200 HTTP requests per minute to an application with more than 50% returning 404 or 403 status codes

Alert on Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF): HTTP requests from the application server to internal IP ranges (RFC 1918) or the metadata service IP not originated by the application's own service account
Detect XML External Entity (XXE) injection: HTTP requests with Content-Type of application/xml or text/xml containing DOCTYPE declarations or SYSTEM keywords in the request body
Alert on credential stuffing against login endpoint: more than 100 POST requests to the authentication endpoint from a single IP within 5 minutes with distinct username values
Detect Log4Shell exploitation attempt: HTTP request headers (User-Agent, X-Forwarded-For, X-Api-Version) containing ${jndi:ldap://, ${jndi:rmi://, or ${jndi:dns:// patterns
Alert on command injection in web application: HTTP requests where parameters contain semicolons, pipe characters, or backticks followed by OS commands such as id, whoami, or cat /etc/passwd
Detect suspicious user agent strings: HTTP requests with User-Agent values matching known scanning tools (sqlmap, nikto, dirbuster, masscan) or empty User-Agent headers to application endpoints

Alert on deserialization attack: HTTP requests with Content-Type of application/x-java-serialized-object or containing aced0005 magic bytes, or PHP unserialize patterns in POST body targeting known vulnerable endpoints

Detect API key exposure in response bodies: application responses containing patterns matching AWS access key format (AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}), JWT tokens, or private key PEM headers in non-authentication endpoints
Alert on HTTP verb tampering: requests using PUT, DELETE, TRACE, or CONNECT methods to endpoints that only expose GET and POST according to the application's API specification
Detect account takeover via session fixation: a session token that was assigned prior to authentication being reused after a successful login event without the server issuing a new session identifier
Detect phishing email delivery: inbound email with a sender domain registered within the last 7 days containing a URL or attachment and targeting more than 5 internal recipients

Alert on business email compromise: an email sent from an external domain that closely resembles an internal domain (edit distance <= 2) with a subject line matching invoice, payment, or wire transfer patterns

Detect email-based credential harvesting: a user clicking a URL in an email that redirects through more than 2 hops to a login page with a domain different from the stated sender domain, followed by a credential submission POST

Alert on malicious attachment delivery: email with attachments of type .xlsm, .docm, .js, .vbs, .lnk, or .iso arriving from external senders to users who have not previously received email from that domain
Detect email forwarding rule to external domain: Exchange or Microsoft 365 audit log showing a mailbox rule or SMTP forwarding configured to redirect mail to a domain outside the corporate tenant

Alert on email reply-chain hijacking: a reply to an existing legitimate email thread from an external lookalike domain where the thread ID matches a real internal conversation, targeting finance or executive recipients
Detect mass spear-phishing campaign: more than 50 inbound emails within 1 hour sharing the same sending IP, similar subject lines, and containing URLs to the same second-level domain

Alert on email with password-protected attachment from first-time external sender: inbound email containing a ZIP, RAR, or PDF with password mentioned in the email body from a domain not previously seen

Detect OAuth phishing via malicious app consent: a user clicking an email link that triggers a Microsoft 365 or Google Workspace OAuth consent dialog for an application requesting Mail.Read, Files.ReadWrite, or equivalent sensitive scopes
Alert on executive impersonation: email with From display name matching a C-level executive but with an external sending domain, lacking DMARC alignment, and requesting urgent financial action
Detect DMARC or SPF failure from spoofed internal domain: inbound email claiming to be from the organisation's own domain that fails SPF and DMARC checks

Alert on HTML smuggling in email: an email attachment or body containing an HTML file that reconstructs a malicious binary using base64-encoded blobs assembled in JavaScript and triggers a file download via Blob URL
Detect email data loss: a user sending an email with attachments classified as confidential or containing patterns matching PII (SSN, credit card numbers) to a recipient outside the corporate domain
Alert on email bombing: a single mailbox receiving more than 500 inbound emails within 10 minutes (potentially masking a password reset or account takeover notification)
Detect QR code phishing: an inbound email containing an embedded image with a QR code that resolves to a credential harvesting domain not listed in the organisation's trusted URL categories
